input.question: We are a student engineering team designing a vehicle for the FSAE competition. Attached is the FSAE rules document. What does rule EV.7.7.4 state exactly? Answer with only the text of the rule and no other words.

retrieved_context:
RULE: ev.7.7.4
            TITLE: the team must have a test to demonstrate bspd operation at electrical technical inspection.

            CONTENT:
            rule EV.7.7.4: The team must have a test to demonstrate BSPD operation at Electrical Technical Inspection.
EV.7.7.4 The team must have a test to demonstrate BSPD operation at Electrical Technical Inspection.   
a. Power must not be sent to the Motor(s) of the vehicle during the test  
b. The test must prove the function of the complete BSPD in the vehicle, including the 
current sensor 
The suggested test would introduce a current by a separate wire from an external power 
supply simulating the Tractive System current while pressing the brake pedal

final_answer: rule EV.7.7.4: The team must have a test to demonstrate BSPD operation at Electrical Technical Inspection. EV.7.7.4 The team must have a test to demonstrate BSPD operation at Electrical Technical Inspection. a. Power must not be sent to the Motor(s) of the vehicle during the test b. The test must prove the function of the complete BSPD in the vehicle, including the current sensor
